headline: 2 Million Documented. 4 Million Estimated. Zero Plan.
context: Malaysia has approximately 2 million documented foreign workers and an estimated 2–4 million undocumented, creating a parallel economy the government neither controls nor acknowledges.

cards.big: One in five workers in Malaysia may be undocumented.
cards.sub: The government's own range estimate is a confession of ignorance.

cards.big: Foreign worker levy revenue: RM3.8B. Undocumented economic output: RM42B.
cards.sub: The informal economy subsidises the formal one while being criminalised by it.

cards.big: Six amnesty programmes since 2011. Each promised to be the last.
cards.sub: Legalise, deport, re-enter, repeat. The cycle is the policy.

cards.big: Employers face RM50,000 fines. Prosecutions in 2023: 214.
cards.sub: Estimated 120,000 violations. The law exists. Enforcement is negligible.

cards.big: Malaysia does not have a foreign worker problem. It has a dependency it refuses to name.
cards.sub: Construction, plantation, and services collapse without this labour force.

cards.big: A nation cannot govern what it refuses to count. The first honest step is a credible census of who is actually here.
